mappings.notes: Agent generates persuasive false information to manipulate human approvals
Agent produces high-volume, fluent content that overwhelms human critical assessment
Compromised agent crafts highly personalised, convincing manipulation targeted at specific users
Policy on agent transparency — agents must identify as AI, advisory outputs clearly distinguished from authoritative
Evaluation of transparency controls — verify advisory labels persist in all interface contexts
Feedback channels detecting operator over-trust patterns — aggregate analysis of agent-influenced decisions
Response for detected trust exploitation patterns — operator retraining, interface redesign, aggregate audit

mappings.control_id: Users informed of capabilities, limitations, and AI nature
mappings.notes: Agents must clearly communicate their AI nature and advisory limitations

mappings.control_id: Effective human oversight over high-risk AI
mappings.notes: Humans must be able to override agent recommendations — trust exploitation undermines Art. 14 effectiveness

mappings.control_id: Chatbots and AI-generated content must disclose AI nature
mappings.control_name: Art. 50 — Transparency for certain AI systems
mappings.notes: All agent-user interactions require AI disclosure — universal obligation

mappings.control_name: Information security awareness, education and training
mappings.notes: All users of agentic decision-support tools trained on AI limitations — verification requirements, how to identify manipulation
Policy on agentic AI advisory use — domains requiring verification, approval flow independence from agent interface
Aggregate over-trust patterns monitored — systematic operator acceptance without verification detected
Agent advisory output classified — users cannot mistake model recommendations for authoritative system content
Trust exploitation impact assessed — which persons are affected by AI decisions influenced by manipulated trust
AI system transparency obligations — users informed of AI nature, advisory status, limitations, EU AI Act Art. 50 alignment
Guidance on appropriate use — domains requiring human verification, how to distinguish AI advisory from authoritative content
Leadership commitment to AI transparency — AI disclosure and advisory labelling requirements in AI policy

mappings.control_name: CIS 14 — Security Awareness and Skills Training
mappings.notes: All users of agentic tools trained on AI limitations — verification requirements, how to identify manipulation
Defined response for trust exploitation incidents — operator retraining, pattern audit, interface redesign
Approval flows independent of agent interface — sensitive approvals cannot be completed via agent chat
Agent-influenced operator decisions logged — aggregate over-trust patterns detectable
AI advisory limitations documented as business logic assumptions — verification requirements per domain
Agent advisory outputs clearly labelled — users cannot mistake AI output for authoritative content

mappings.control_name: Verify API rejects large unexpected payloads
mappings.notes: Approval flows independent of agent interface — no state-changing approvals via agent chat
LLM advisory outputs clearly distinguished from authoritative documentation — source always visible
Procedures for detecting operator over-trust patterns — aggregate analysis of agent-influenced decisions
Agent recommendations for safety-relevant decisions cross-validated against independent reference
Inadequate human oversight cited as OT vulnerability category
Quantify consequences of unsupervised agent OT actions
Governance policy for autonomous OT systems
Policy requires AI disclosure and advisory labelling — agent transparency as a governance requirement
Users trained on AI limitations — operators understand agent advisory status and verification requirements
Monitoring for aggregate over-trust patterns — systematic operator acceptance without verification detected
Information shared following incidents — trust exploitation incidents reported to affected users

mappings.control_id: AI disclosure policy — users informed when interacting with AI agents; deceptive design patterns prohibited
mappings.notes: AI disclosure policy, UI evidence

mappings.control_id: Trust exploitation risk in risk assessment — social engineering, impersonation, false urgency scenarios documented
mappings.notes: Risk register with trust exploitation entries

mappings.control_id: Agent outputs are accurate and complete — outputs not designed to mislead; factual accuracy controls for high-stakes outputs
mappings.notes: Output quality controls, factual accuracy testing

mappings.control_id: Accuracy of personal data in AI outputs — privacy criteria require that AI-generated information about individuals is accurate
mappings.notes: Accuracy review procedures

mappings.control_id: Security awareness covers AI deception — staff trained to recognise AI impersonation and social engineering
mappings.notes: Training curriculum, completion records

mappings.control_id: Honest design requirements for agent interactions — AI disclosure requirements in secure development policy
mappings.notes: Secure development policy, design review records

mappings.control_id: Agent-human interaction events logged — session records for agent interactions involving CHD decisions
mappings.notes: Interaction audit log

mappings.control_id: Trust exploitation risk analysis — targeted risk analysis documents scenarios and treatment
mappings.notes: Risk analysis documentation
Users informed when interacting with AI agents — disclosure requirements, impersonation prohibitions, accountability for agent outputs
AI system integrity testing includes deceptive output scenarios — social engineering, false urgency, impersonation tested before deployment
Agent outputs monitored for deceptive content patterns — AI-specific detection for impersonation and social engineering indicators
Acceptable use policy covers agent interaction design — deceptive design patterns prohibited
Define maximum autonomy scope per agent class; mandate oversight gates
Design confirmation gate pattern for all high-impact actions
Alert when agent executes high-impact actions without confirmation
Immutable audit log of all autonomous actions for post-hoc review
Operators understand when to intervene and how to trigger override
System accepts human-like agent output without disclosure requirements

mappings.control_id: User Interface Misrepresentation of Critical Information
mappings.notes: Agent advisory output not clearly distinguished from authoritative system information in UI
User cannot determine whether advice originates from AI or human — no origin verification

mappings.control_id: Improper Verification of Source of a Communication Channel
mappings.notes: User cannot verify the agent is operating under its stated configuration

mappings.control_id: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorised Actor
mappings.notes: System prompt and agent configuration not protected — extraction enables targeted trust exploitation
Verify agent identifies as AI in all interface contexts; test for identity concealment
Verify advisory labels persist through all rendering environments
Verify monitoring can detect aggregate patterns of uncritical operator acceptance

mappings.control_id: Humans using agent credentials — or agents using human credentials — destroys attribution
mappings.notes: Strict separation: agent credentials machine-only, human credentials human-only

mappings.control_id: Over-privileged agent identity makes its recommendations appear more authoritative to users
mappings.notes: Least privilege makes agent capabilities visible and bounded — users understand what the agent can actually do

mappings.control_id: Review agent behaviour for emergent capabilities — verify that self-modification, dynamic tool discovery, and autonomous agent spawning do not create unintended security exposures
mappings.control_name: PW.7.2-PS – Review the software for security vulnerabilities
mappings.notes: Catches emergent pattern risks before production

mappings.control_id: Conduct adversarial testing targeting emerging agentic patterns — self-evolution, prompt self-modification, autonomous tool acquisition, and dynamic agent creation
mappings.notes: Validates controls against novel attack surfaces

mappings.control_id: Establish monitoring for emergent agent behaviours — detect agents acquiring new capabilities, modifying their own definitions, or spawning sub-agents outside approved patterns
mappings.notes: Enables detection of emerging risks in production

mappings.control_id: When incidents involve novel agentic patterns, conduct root cause analysis focused on understanding the emergent capability and its security implications
mappings.notes: Builds organisational knowledge of emerging agentic risks
Include monitoring for novel agentic behaviour patterns in FedRAMP continuous monitoring; track for unexpected capabilities, emergent behaviours, and architectural drift
Include emerging agentic attack surfaces in vulnerability scanning; assess novel architectures for security implications before production deployment
Monitor agent systems for unexpected behaviour — novel tool use patterns, unanticipated goal decomposition, and emergent inter-agent coordination
Conduct regular audit review of agent decision logs; identify patterns indicative of unexpected capabilities or emergent behaviours requiring security assessment

mappings.control_name: ICT Risk Management — novel agentic risk governance

mappings.control_name: Learning and Evolving — emerging pattern assessment
mappings.notes: Apply lessons learned from emerging agentic pattern incidents; update governance and controls as new attack techniques and defence approaches emerge
